channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Republicans ask party to keep clean energy credits
item.description: The Republicans who signed the letter have not ruled out voting against a continuing resolution to fund the government.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson looks to jam Senate Democrats with bill to avert shutdown
item.description: Before Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) can dare Senate Democrats to reject a Republican-crafted bill to avert an end-of-week shutdown, he has to pull off a feat: keeping his fractious, razor-thin majority united behind the legislation.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street's sell-off worsens after Trump hikes tariffs
item.description: The U.S. stock market is sinking further Tuesday after President Donald Trump raised the stakes in his trade war, pulling Wall Street more than 9% below its record set just a month ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First on Fox: Top conservative group aligns with Trump as it makes major endorsement in high-profile race
item.description: The Club for Growth, a top conservative group that is a major player in Republican primaries, aligns with President Trump as it endorses Byron Donalds in the race to succeed Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The government already knows a lot about you. DOGE is trying to access all of it
item.description: Agencies from Social Security to the IRS store sensitive data on millions of Americans. Here’s what the government knows about us — and what’s at risk as DOGE seeks access to the data.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: Republicans are marching ahead with a government funding bill despite Democratic opposition
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson says lawmakers "have to keep the government in operation” as Republicans face a critical test of their unity.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kentucky bourbon makers fear becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's trade war
item.description: The iconic American spirit's widening global appeal is caught in the crossfire of trade conflicts instigated by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Monday the Trump administration had finished its six-week purge of programs of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development, cutting 83% of them, and said he would move the remaining aid programs under the State Department.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration halts funding for two cybersecurity efforts, including one for elections
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Trump administration has cut millions of dollars in federal funding from two cybersecurity initiatives, including one dedicated to helping state and local election officials. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks deportation of Columbia University protester
item.description: A Palestinian green card holder, Khalil earned his master's degree in December, and his wife, a U.S. citizen, is eight months pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Immigration needs to be 'shut down' until unity restored: Carlson
item.description: President Donald Trump has pledged history's largest domestic deportation, but some fear how long the process could take
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Confronting backlash against women's rights, 193 nations commit to speed action on gender equality
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Confronting a rising backlash against women’s rights, the U.N.’s 193 member nations made a commitment Monday to accelerate action on more than a dozen fronts to achieve gender equality. A political declaration adopted at the start of the annual meeting of the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whiskey business still feeling effects of 2018 Trump tariffs
item.description: "Every single time there's retaliatory tariffs, it's bourbon that gets hit," one business owner says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: EPA froze 'green bank' funds worth billions, climate group suit says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nonprofit that was awarded nearly $7 billion by the Biden administration to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects has sued President Donald Trump's Environmental Protection Agency, accusing it of improperly freezing a legally awarded grant.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: "Gross abuses of executive power raise serious concerns over the freedoms of peaceful assembly, expression and association," the group wrote of the U.S. rating.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration cancels $400M in grants and contracts with Columbia University
item.description: Multiple federal agencies announced Friday that they're canceling grants and contracts because of what the government describes as the Ivy League school’s lackluster efforts to squelch antisemitism on campus. The U of M is one of five colleges under new federal antisemitism investigations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Students rally at Florida capitol opposing amendment petition reform
item.description: From the Panhandle to the Keys, hundreds of students from across Florida protested Thursday at the state capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Transgender staffer navigates Texas Capitol with guarded care amid growing hostilities
item.description: Mo Jenkins is one of only a few openly transgender staffers working in the Capitol as lawmakers are making headway in restricting rights for LGBTQ+ Texans.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hillsborough commission votes against asking for state DOGE audit
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The newly formed Florida State Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) task force is set to use technology and AI to help look for government waste. It will audit colleges and universities, state agencies, along with local governments.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
